ops.title: For cc-notes CLI/store semantics the user prefers simple best-effort guards over locking or convergence machinery — rejected an flock-serialized dedupe in favor of a plain scan-before-create on every kind
ops.body:
Planning the duplicate-note fix, a race-correct flock design (serialize scan→create, kernel-released on death) was proposed; the user rejected it: "we dont need locks, just have every `create` for any object check if there's a duplicate as best-effort".

**Why:** cc-notes is a local single-user tool; a rare residual race window is an acceptable cost for keeping the primitive simple, and the guard belongs generally on every entity kind rather than precisely on the one observed producer.

**How to apply:** when designing guards for cc-notes (and similar house tools), default to the simple best-effort check applied uniformly; reach for locks/CAS/convergence protocols only when the user asks for hard guarantees. Generalize the cheap check across kinds instead of deep-engineering one path. Related: [[uses-jj-locally-sometimes]] (same taste: explicit simple mechanisms over clever machinery).
